text: The fabric shops in Koreatown are gone now, but I remember going with my grandmother when
Those moments will never leave my mind.
We'd walk in and say nothing, then the shopkeeper would begin.
Bolt after bolt, her face never changed.
I learned to watch her jaw, a tightening meant no, and the bolt would disappear.
Sometimes a pattern would catch her eye and influence her next decision.
Or a color would call her.
She'd want to try something else.
And sometimes she just wanted to see more.
And then she knew, could go.
The fabric shop is gone, but that feeling isn't.
